must_haves:
  truths:
    - A committed live-probe artifact answers the diagnosis question FIRST —
      `claude -p` DOES write a session record to its cwd-keyed store, with a
      top-level cwd, an ISO timestamp and a complete message.usage — so SPEND-11
      closes as a FIX, not a won't-implement (ROADMAP SC-1, D-01/D-04/D-05).
    - "`collectUsage` reports billed tokens for a headless `-p` worker in a
      WORKTREE-SHAPED cwd (the only shape drover ever dispatches into), pinned
      by a test that is RED against HEAD because the store slug is computed
      wrong (ROADMAP SC-2, D-06)."
    - "The reported number is the BILLED number, not a multiple of it: claude
      splits one assistant response across N records, each repeating the full
      usage — dedup by message.id lands WITH the slug fix, so metering never
      trades an honest `unmetered` for a 2x fabricated fact (ROADMAP SC-3,
      D-08)."
    - Dedup runs AFTER the cursor filter, so a pre-`sinceMs` record can never
      poison the seen-set and suppress the record that is actually being billed.
    - The store path a test plants at is an INDEPENDENT LITERAL, never
      re-derived from the code under test — the existing tautological `slugDir`
      helper (claude-usage.test.ts:33), which planted the fixture at exactly the
      place the buggy code looked, is repaired (D-03; the v1.7 SPEND-03 scar).
    - "The RED observation is a COMMITTED ARTIFACT, not a worker claim: the
      literal vitest failure output from running the new oracles against unfixed
      HEAD lives under '## RED proof (D-02)' in 36-DIAGNOSIS.md, where a gate
      greps it (D-02, D-18)."
